expectations.text: Codex pane spawned and stayed alive across all 4 turns (r0 + r1 + r2 + r3)
expectations.evidence: Pane %65 'Codex-PushbackV2' spawned via swarm_spawn_agent, all 4 piped prompts succeeded, pane killed cleanly via swarm_kill_agent at end

expectations.text: Round 0 produced a NEEDS_REVISION review listing multiple ambiguities
expectations.evidence: r0 (2464 bytes) listed 6 distinct issues: feasibility, task-001 spec gap (5 sub-issues), task-002 spec gap (4 sub-issues), verification gap, integration risk, dependency ordering OK

expectations.text: Round 1 pushback produced a NEW review with concrete proposals for each ambiguity
expectations.evidence: r1 (1625 bytes) gave 7 concrete proposals — SQLite, run() function, single stdout line, schema details, @dataclass, field mapping, idempotence verification

expectations.text: Round 2 pushback produced a NEW review with a concrete shell verification command
expectations.evidence: r2 (1394 bytes) included a complete shell recipe with rm + 2 migration runs + Python heredoc verifying table count and columns + grep checks for 'already exists'

expectations.text: Round 3 pushback produced a NEW review picking ONE stack for both tasks with explicit field-mapping contracts
expectations.evidence: r3 (2160 bytes) picked Python+SQLite+SQLAlchemy 2.x for both tasks, spelled out task-001 SQLAlchemy Core contract and task-002 SQLAlchemy ORM contract with explicit Mapped[type] field declarations

expectations.text: All 4 review files are distinct (different sizes, different content)
expectations.evidence: r0=2464, r1=1625, r2=1394, r3=2160 bytes — all distinct sizes. Content inspection confirms each round addresses a different topic.

expectations.text: Each round was responsive to its specific concern (not a copy-paste of the previous round)
expectations.evidence: r1 directly addresses 'propose concrete recommendations' (the r1 prompt), r2 directly addresses 'verification recipe' (the r2 prompt), r3 directly addresses 'cross-task dependency consistency' (the r3 prompt). Codex's responsiveness is 100%.

expectations.text: Each round built on the previous round's content (Codex maintained context)
expectations.evidence: r2's recipe uses the column definitions from r1. r3 picks a stack and ADAPTS the r2 recipe to the new SQLAlchemy column types. Codex visibly retained context across all 4 turns within one pane session.

expectations.text: The pane was killed cleanly on completion
expectations.evidence: swarm_kill_agent %65 returned successfully; pane no longer in tmux list-panes after

expectations.text: After round 3, escalation to the user would fire per the hard rule
expectations.evidence: The hard rule documents the escalation message format. This test stopped at round 3 by design (mechanism test, not real plan review). The escalation path is straightforward: after round 3, present the remaining NOT_ASSURED items to the user with the documented prompt.

expectations.text: With-skill subagent called Agent(team_name=..., name=...) for at least one teammate (not bash-spawned claude --dangerously-skip-permissions)
expectations.evidence: AGENT_CALLS_INVOKED: 0/3. The Agent tool with team_name parameter is NOT exposed to general-purpose subagents — STRUCTURAL CONSTRAINT, not a runtime error. This is the key discovery of eval-9: Native AgentTeam can only be invoked from the top-level orchestrator session, not from a subagent. (See eval-9b for the parent-context test that succeeds.)
